title: fix: h100-mfu-calculation
body: I was getting 104% MFU on h100 then i realized that MFU calculation might of been based on a100 312 tflops h100 is 989 tflops at bfloat16. nvidia claims 1,979tflops but that is for 2:4 sparsity.

files_modified.patch:
@@ -298,7 +298,10 @@ def estimate_mfu(self, fwdbwd_per_iter, dt):
         flops_per_iter = flops_per_fwdbwd * fwdbwd_per_iter
         # express our flops throughput as ratio of A100 bfloat16 peak flops
         flops_achieved = flops_per_iter * (1.0/dt) # per second
-        flops_promised = 312e12 # A100 GPU bfloat16 peak flops is 312 TFLOPS
+        if torch.cuda.get_device_name() == 'NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3':
+            flops_promised = 989e12 # H100 GPU bfloat16 peak flops is 989 TFLOPs
+        else:
+            flops_promised = 312e12 # A100 GPU bfloat16 peak flops is 312 TFLOPS
         mfu = flops_achieved / flops_promised
         return mfu

steps.operation: Adjust MFU normalization to use H100 BF16 peak FLOPs when running on an NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3, otherwise default to A100 peak FLOPs
steps.reason: The MFU metric was previously normalized against A100 (312 TFLOPs BF16) for all GPUs, which causes inflated/incorrect MFU values (e.g., >100%) on H100 hardware; using H100’s ~989 TFLOPs BF16 peak provides a correct baseline.
steps.side_effects: MFU values reported/logged on H100 will drop to reflect the higher theoretical peak; device-name string matching may fail on other H100 variants or different name formats (leading to fallback A100 baseline and inaccurate MFU), potentially affecting monitoring/benchmark comparisons across heterogeneous GPU setups.

step_plan.summary: Fixes MFU calculation by normalizing achieved FLOPs against the correct BF16 peak throughput on NVIDIA H100 (989 TFLOPs) instead of always using the A100 baseline (312 TFLOPs).
